name: Black Canchal via Las Cobatillas – circular running in the Sierra de Candelario Natural Space
name_original: Canchal Negro por Las Cobatillas – running circular por el Espacio Natural de la Sierra de Candelario
region_intro: Jogging around Navacarros offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by its position within natural spaces and proximity to reservoirs and historical towns. The region features varied terrain, from the elevated areas of the Sierra de Candelario Natural Space to routes offering views of the Embalse de Navamuño. These paths provide opportunities to experience both natural beauty and cultural landmarks. Best jogging routes around Navacarros The most popular jogging route is Black Canchal via Las Cobatillas – circular running in the Sierra de Candelario Natural Space, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 38 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging run through a significant natural area. Another top favourite among local runners is La Ancianita bullring – Views on the embalse de Navamuño loop from Béjar, a difficult 10.1 miles (16.3 km) path. This route provides scenic views of the reservoir and passes by a local bullring. Local runners also love the Plaza Mayor of Béjar – Ventorro Pelayo Viewpoint loop from Béjar, a 11.7 miles (18.8 km) trail leading through urban and natural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 4 minutes. Jogging around Navacarros is defined by natural spaces, reservoirs, and viewpoints, offering a mix of challenging and moderate routes. The network provides options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more difficult trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Navacarros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Navacarros?
A: There are over 60 running routes around Navacarros documented on komoot, offering a wide range of options for different fitness levels and preferences.

Q: Are there easy running paths for beginners in Navacarros?
A: While the region is known for its challenging terrain, there is at least one easy running route available. The majority of routes are classified as moderate or difficult, so beginners should carefully check route details before heading out.

Q: What are the typical lengths of running routes in the Navacarros area?
A: Running routes around Navacarros vary significantly in length. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993681601/black-canchal-via-las-cobatillas-circular-running-in-the-sierra-de-candelario-natural-space" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Black Canchal via Las Cobatillas&lt;/a&gt; route is about 8.7 km (5.4 miles), while longer options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canchal de la Ceja – Torreón (Calvitero Summit) loop&lt;/a&gt; extend to over 42 km (26 miles).

Q: Are there any circular running trails near Navacarros?
A: Yes, many running routes in the area are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993681601/black-canchal-via-las-cobatillas-circular-running-in-the-sierra-de-candelario-natural-space" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Black Canchal via Las Cobatillas – circular running in the Sierra de Candelario Natural Space&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a challenging loop through a significant natural area.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on running trails around Navacarros?
A: The running trails around Navacarros offer diverse scenery, from the elevated areas of the Sierra de Candelario Natural Space to routes with views of the Embalse de Navamuño. You can expect a mix of natural beauty, reservoirs, and cultural landmarks.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions along the running routes?
A: Yes, several routes pass by notable landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31867934" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Ancianita bullring – Views on the embalse de Navamuño loop from Béjar&lt;/a&gt; route offers scenic views of the reservoir and passes a local bullring. You might also encounter highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6255607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Béjar Station and Vía de la Plata Tunnel&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1062938" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Humilladero&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find running routes with viewpoints in Navacarros?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer stunning viewpoints, showcasing the region's natural beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Béjar – Ventorro Pelayo Viewpoint loop from Béjar&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading through urban and natural landscapes with scenic vistas.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Navacarros?
A: The komoot community highly values the diverse landscape and the mix of challenging and moderate routes available. Runners often praise the opportunities to experience both natural beauty and cultural landmarks, with over 300 users having explored the varied terrain.

Q: Are there running routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region features routes that offer views of reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31870586" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navamuño Reservoir – Candelario Town Hall loop from Candelario&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option for those looking to run alongside water, providing views of the Navamuño Reservoir.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Navacarros?
A: Navacarros offers a diverse landscape that can be enjoyed throughout the year. Spring and autumn generally provide milder temperatures, ideal for longer runs. Summer mornings are also pleasant, while winter can offer unique, crisp running conditions, especially in higher elevations.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes in Navacarros?
A: While many routes are classified as difficult, there is at least one easy route available, which might be suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous run. It's always recommended to check the specific route details and difficulty before planning a family outing.

Q: Are there options for long-distance running or trail running in Navacarros?
A: Yes, Navacarros is well-suited for long-distance and trail running, with 42 routes classified as difficult. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canchal de la Ceja – Torreón (Calvitero Summit) loop from Béjar&lt;/a&gt;, at over 42 km, is a prime example of a challenging, long-distance trail run in the area.
tips: Canchal Negro is a point in Salamanca and has an elevation of 2,369 meters. It is located to the east of Sierra de Candelario.
